Agent.description: Capability of acting, deciding, or bearing responsibility. Aligned with PROV:Agent and BDI model. Agency is a trait, not a taxonomic position: an entity CAN act, it is not defined by acting. Apply this mixin to any entity class that needs volitional behavior.

agent_type.description: Classification of the agent. Domain projects should constrain this to an enum.

Agent.title: Agent

Chunk.description: An ingested chunk of memory from a MemoryProvider. Source-of-truth for provenance; referenced by Concept via HasProvenance edges.

t_source_created.description: When the user wrote the underlying content.

Chunk.title: Chunk

Concept.description: An extracted topic, theme, entity, question, decision, or tension. Statusable tracks ACTIVE / deprecated lifecycle; other mixins come from Entity.

canonical_id.description: When two concepts are resolved to the same entity, the non-canonical one points here at the canonical id. Null for active canonicals.

frequency.description: How many times this concept has been observed.

source_quote.description: The user's exact words that demonstrate this concept.

Concept.title: Concept

Enriches.description: Connects a user-facing node to external knowledge (ADR 011 v2). FROM spans Concept / Wisdom for now; consumer ontologies extend via slot_usage delta to include their own typed nodes.

gate_verdicts.description: JSON array of per-gate verdicts {plugin, score, outcome}.

relation_type.description: The type of relation (has_part, contains, depends_on, etc.). Domain projects should constrain this to an enum.

relevance.description: Final score from the gate sequence (0..1).

strength.description: Weight or confidence of the relation (0.0 to 1.0).

Enriches.title: Enriches

EnrichmentRun.description: Telemetry for a single enrichment invocation (ADR 011 v2). One node per trigger-fire-per-source-per-concept. Queryable via et_shift(node_types=["EnrichmentRun"]) so users and algorithms can tune thresholds based on real data rather than guesswork.

caused_by.description: ID of the event or agent that caused this event. Follows PROV-O wasInformedBy / wasAssociatedWith pattern.

concept_id.description: The user-facing node whose context fired the enrichment.

error.description: Non-empty if the run failed (e.g. Wikipedia 5xx). Enables retry queries.

event_type.description: Classification of the event. Domain projects should constrain this to an enum.

EnrichmentRun.title: EnrichmentRun

Entity.description: Root class for all identifiable, enduring things. Aligned with BFO:Independent Continuant. Every domain object that persists through time and needs identity extends this.
Entity.title: Entity

EntityStatus.description: Lifecycle state of any identifiable entity.
EntityStatus.title: EntityStatus

Event.description: Something that happens at a point or over an interval. Aligned with BFO:Occurrent / PROV:Activity. Instantaneous events use occurred_at. Processes with duration use started_at / ended_at (Allen's interval algebra).

caused_by.description: ID of the event or agent that caused this event. Follows PROV-O wasInformedBy / wasAssociatedWith pattern.

event_type.description: Classification of the event. Domain projects should constrain this to an enum.

Event.title: Event

Fragment.description: A discrete piece of content inside a Session — a message, code block, note, or command. The atomic unit of captured thinking.

role.description: Role of the author (for conversational fragments).

Fragment.title: Fragment

HasProvenance.description: Concept-to-Chunk provenance edge. Records which chunk (and which LLM extraction pass) produced this concept.

llm_model.description: Model id that performed the extraction.

relation_type.description: The type of relation (has_part, contains, depends_on, etc.). Domain projects should constrain this to an enum.

source_provider.description: Name of the MemoryProvider that supplied the chunk.

strength.description: Weight or confidence of the relation (0.0 to 1.0).

HasProvenance.title: HasProvenance

Identifiable.description: Anything with a stable, globally unique identity. Aligned with BFO Independent Continuant: exists on its own, bears qualities, participates in processes.
Identifiable.title: Identifiable

InformedBy.description: A Wisdom is informed by Concepts. Provenance trail for a synthesized insight back to its evidence.

relation_type.description: The type of relation (has_part, contains, depends_on, etc.). Domain projects should constrain this to an enum.

strength.description: Weight or confidence of the relation (0.0 to 1.0).

InformedBy.title: InformedBy

Insight.description: A detected pattern, connection, recurrence, or evolution. A Signal because it is a derived quality that emerges from patterns of events (extractions, relationships) across Entities (concepts, sessions).

algorithm.description: Name or reference of the computation that produces this signal. Domain projects should document algorithms and constrain this to an enum. Examples: appleseed, pagerank, ewma, linear_decay.

bearer_id.description: ID of the entity (or relationship) this signal inheres in. Required because a Signal cannot exist without a bearer (BFO: dependent continuant).

computed_at.description: When this signal value was last computed.

perspective.description: ID of the entity from whose perspective this signal is computed. Null for global/objective signals (e.g., graph density). Set for subjective signals (e.g., trust computed from one agent's viewpoint).

signal_type.description: Classification of the signal. Domain projects should constrain this to an enum. Examples: trust_score, health_score, centrality.

value.description: Current computed value. Interpretation is signal-type specific. Often 0.0–1.0 but not constrained at the root level (domain decides range and semantics).

Insight.title: Insight

KnowledgeNode.description: An external knowledge item (Wikipedia article, arXiv paper, Fowler refactoring, etc.) attached to a user-facing concept. Signal because it is a derived quality — the source's representation of a topic — inhering in its bearer concept via Signal.bearer_id. Namespace convention: `enrichment:<source_kind>` (ADR 011 v2).

abstract.description: Text representation indexed for semantic recall.

algorithm.description: Name or reference of the computation that produces this signal. Domain projects should document algorithms and constrain this to an enum. Examples: appleseed, pagerank, ewma, linear_decay.

bearer_id.description: ID of the entity (or relationship) this signal inheres in. Required because a Signal cannot exist without a bearer (BFO: dependent continuant).

computed_at.description: When this signal value was last computed.

external_id.description: Source-native id (Wikidata QID, arXiv ID, Fowler slug).

perspective.description: ID of the entity from whose perspective this signal is computed. Null for global/objective signals (e.g., graph density). Set for subjective signals (e.g., trust computed from one agent's viewpoint).

signal_type.description: Classification of the signal. Domain projects should constrain this to an enum. Examples: trust_score, health_score, centrality.

theme.description: JSON array of theme tags for sub-classification within a source. Multi-theme membership allowed. Source plugins populate this (Wikipedia via LLM classifier, arXiv via native category IDs, etc.). Empty means unclassified.

value.description: Current computed value. Interpretation is signal-type specific. Often 0.0–1.0 but not constrained at the root level (domain decides range and semantics).

KnowledgeNode.title: KnowledgeNode

Platform.description: Source platform where content was captured.
Platform.title: Platform

ProposalBy.description: Algorithm write-back edge (ADR 013 C7). Records that at time T, plugin P proposed the source→target connection with score S under scope parameters Q. Distinct from committed edges like RelatesTo: a proposal captures what the algorithm said, not what the user or consumer accepted. For MVP this is pinned Concept→Concept; other endpoint pairs land as new subclasses when a consumer needs them.

parameters_json.description: JSON-encoded parameters used for this invocation.

relation_type.description: The type of relation (has_part, contains, depends_on, etc.). Domain projects should constrain this to an enum.

score.description: Algorithm-specific score attached to this proposal.

strength.description: Weight or confidence of the relation (0.0 to 1.0).

ProposalBy.title: ProposalBy

Rationale.description: An LLM-generated justification attached to a subject node. Uses malleus Signal's bearer_id to point at the subject; cited_node_ids enumerates the evidence. The grounded-rationale guarantee (ADR 013 C4 / R8) requires every citation to resolve before commit — an ungrounded rationale cannot enter the graph.

algorithm.description: Name or reference of the computation that produces this signal. Domain projects should document algorithms and constrain this to an enum. Examples: appleseed, pagerank, ewma, linear_decay.

bearer_id.description: ID of the entity (or relationship) this signal inheres in. Required because a Signal cannot exist without a bearer (BFO: dependent continuant).

cited_node_ids.description: JSON array of node ids the rationale cites as evidence.

computed_at.description: When this signal value was last computed.

perspective.description: ID of the entity from whose perspective this signal is computed. Null for global/objective signals (e.g., graph density). Set for subjective signals (e.g., trust computed from one agent's viewpoint).

signal_type.description: Classification of the signal. Domain projects should constrain this to an enum. Examples: trust_score, health_score, centrality.

text.description: The rationale text, verbatim as produced by the LLM.

value.description: Current computed value. Interpretation is signal-type specific. Often 0.0–1.0 but not constrained at the root level (domain decides range and semantics).

Rationale.title: Rationale

RelatesTo.description: Concept-to-concept semantic relation. Weighted, with optional context quote describing why they relate. Primary edge in the cognitive graph.

access_count.description: Number of times this edge has been traversed. Drives Physarum decay — frequently traversed edges stay strong, idle edges fade.

last_accessed.description: Timestamp of the most recent access.

relation_type.description: The type of relation (has_part, contains, depends_on, etc.). Domain projects should constrain this to an enum.

strength.description: Weight or confidence of the relation (0.0 to 1.0).

RelatesTo.title: RelatesTo

Relation.description: A typed, directed edge between two entities. Reified as a class so relations can carry metadata (strength, confidence, temporal validity).

relation_type.description: The type of relation (has_part, contains, depends_on, etc.). Domain projects should constrain this to an enum.

strength.description: Weight or confidence of the relation (0.0 to 1.0).

Relation.title: Relation

Session.description: A conversation or interaction session — an Event because it happens over an interval, has a cause chain, and bounds a coherent episode.

caused_by.description: ID of the event or agent that caused this event. Follows PROV-O wasInformedBy / wasAssociatedWith pattern.

event_type.description: Classification of the event. Domain projects should constrain this to an enum.

source_id.description: ID of the Source this session came from.

Session.title: Session

Signal.description: A continuously derived quality that emerges from patterns of Events between Entities. Aligned with BFO:Specifically Dependent Continuant (Quality) and SSN/SOSA:Observation. A Signal inheres in its bearer(s) — it does not exist independently. It is computed, not asserted: derived on demand from the Event log and Entity graph via a named algorithm. Domain projects define signal types, algorithms, and interpretation semantics. The Signal class captures the universal pattern: something measurable that emerges from activity, has a current value, and is recomputable from the underlying data.

algorithm.description: Name or reference of the computation that produces this signal. Domain projects should document algorithms and constrain this to an enum. Examples: appleseed, pagerank, ewma, linear_decay.

bearer_id.description: ID of the entity (or relationship) this signal inheres in. Required because a Signal cannot exist without a bearer (BFO: dependent continuant).

computed_at.description: When this signal value was last computed.

perspective.description: ID of the entity from whose perspective this signal is computed. Null for global/objective signals (e.g., graph density). Set for subjective signals (e.g., trust computed from one agent's viewpoint).

signal_type.description: Classification of the signal. Domain projects should constrain this to an enum. Examples: trust_score, health_score, centrality.

value.description: Current computed value. Interpretation is signal-type specific. Often 0.0–1.0 but not constrained at the root level (domain decides range and semantics).

Signal.title: Signal

Source.description: A capture source — where data was imported or streamed from (a user's ~/.claude/projects, a ChatGPT export, etc.).

captured_at.description: When this source was first captured.

url.description: Optional URL or path to the source.

Source.title: Source

SourceType.description: Classification of a chunk's source material.
SourceType.title: SourceType

Suggestion.description: A proactive recommendation — something the system thinks the user should explore, revisit, connect, or reconsider.

algorithm.description: Name or reference of the computation that produces this signal. Domain projects should document algorithms and constrain this to an enum. Examples: appleseed, pagerank, ewma, linear_decay.

bearer_id.description: ID of the entity (or relationship) this signal inheres in. Required because a Signal cannot exist without a bearer (BFO: dependent continuant).

computed_at.description: When this signal value was last computed.

perspective.description: ID of the entity from whose perspective this signal is computed. Null for global/objective signals (e.g., graph density). Set for subjective signals (e.g., trust computed from one agent's viewpoint).

signal_type.description: Classification of the signal. Domain projects should constrain this to an enum. Examples: trust_score, health_score, centrality.

value.description: Current computed value. Interpretation is signal-type specific. Often 0.0–1.0 but not constrained at the root level (domain decides range and semantics).

Suggestion.title: Suggestion

Supersedes.description: A concept supersedes another — e.g. the user changed their mind and the older stance is deprecated. Drives the t_superseded_by bitemporal pointer for truth maintenance (ADR 002).

reason.description: Why the newer concept supersedes the older one.

relation_type.description: The type of relation (has_part, contains, depends_on, etc.). Domain projects should constrain this to an enum.

strength.description: Weight or confidence of the relation (0.0 to 1.0).

Supersedes.title: Supersedes

Temporal.description: Timestamps for creation and last modification. Follows PROV-O temporal patterns.
Temporal.title: Temporal

Wisdom.description: A synthesized wisdom card generated by the Opus pass over the graph. Statusable because wisdoms flow pending → seen → dismissed.

algorithm.description: Name or reference of the computation that produces this signal. Domain projects should document algorithms and constrain this to an enum. Examples: appleseed, pagerank, ewma, linear_decay.

bearer_id.description: ID of the entity (or relationship) this signal inheres in. Required because a Signal cannot exist without a bearer (BFO: dependent continuant).

computed_at.description: When this signal value was last computed.

perspective.description: ID of the entity from whose perspective this signal is computed. Null for global/objective signals (e.g., graph density). Set for subjective signals (e.g., trust computed from one agent's viewpoint).

related_concept_ids.description: JSON array of concept ids this wisdom cites.

signal_type.description: Classification of the signal. Domain projects should constrain this to an enum. Examples: trust_score, health_score, centrality.

value.description: Current computed value. Interpretation is signal-type specific. Often 0.0–1.0 but not constrained at the root level (domain decides range and semantics).

Wisdom.title: Wisdom

WisdomEnriches.description: Wisdom → KnowledgeNode variant of Enriches. Separate subclass because Kuzu's REL DDL requires FROM/TO types declared per REL table. Future consumer subclasses (HypothesisEnriches etc.) follow the same pattern (ADR 011 v2 multi-pair decision).

relation_type.description: The type of relation (has_part, contains, depends_on, etc.). Domain projects should constrain this to an enum.

strength.description: Weight or confidence of the relation (0.0 to 1.0).

WisdomEnriches.title: WisdomEnriches
